title: Bitter Juicy Argentinian Beef Skewers
description: Tender beef skewers marinated in a mixture of olive oil, vinegar, and herbs, then broiled to perfection, evoking the nostalgic flavors of Argentina. This dish is FODMAP-friendly and perfect for a summer dinner.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, red wine vinegar, chimichurri sauce, garlic-infused oil, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper.
Add the beef cubes to the marinade and mix well to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
If using bay leaves, add them to the marinade for the last 30 minutes.
Thread the marinated beef cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Broil the skewers for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 130°F (54°C) for medium-rare.
Let the skewers rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with additional chimichurri sauce and bay leaves if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for up to 3 days.
Reheat the skewers in the oven at 350°F (180°C) for 5-7 minutes, or until warmed through.
